rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER BORGZINNER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 9, 2013, Line 4, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, LI HUANG'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The motion for summary judgment is denied. The motion for summary adjudication is granted in part (issues 2 and 3) and denied in part (issues 1, 4, 5, 6). There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the off duty Muni operators were acting in the course and scope of their employment and whether they breached their duty of care. (Issue 1) The off-duty officers were uniformed, went to the bus yard prior to boarding the bus, and rode for free. There is a factual issue whether their conduct was in furtherance of Defendant's enterprise. See DeMirjian v. Ideal Heating Corp. (1954) 129 Cal. App. 2d 758, 765. The Court grants summary adjudication on the dangerous condition of public property cause of action. (Issues 2 and 3) Plaintiff fails to come forward with any evidence as to any defect with the bus; and Issue 3, regarding design immunity, is thus moot. Summary adjudication on the negligent training cause of action (issue 4) is denied. It cannot be said as a matter of law that there is no such cause of action under the facts of this case. See C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High School District (2012) 53 Cal. 4th 861. As to issue 5, there is a triable issue of material fact as to defendant Huang's negligence. The incident took approximately 20 minutes (Plaintiff's decl. para 16); Huang admits his mirror was positioned to focus on the front of the bus (Huang Depo., pp. 25-26), and there is a factual issue whether he should have known that an assault was about to occur. As to issue 6, there is a triable issue as to whether off duty personnel breached a duty. The off duty personnel were sitting near Plaintiff and observed the whole exchange. (Plaintiff's decl. paras. 10, 11, 13, 19.) (Part 1 of 2 of the tentative ruling). =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER BORGZINNER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco, Li Huang Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 9, 2013, Line 4, Defendants City And County Of San Francisco, Li Huang's Motion For Summary Judgment (Part 2 of 2 of the tentative ruling). The Court notes that Plaintiff's opposition to the motion does not comply with the Rules of Court. Plaintiff's Response to Defendant's Separate State of Undisputed Facts in Support of Motion for Summary Adjudication improperly and repeatedly incorporates material by reference, rather than "stat[ing] on the right side of the page directly opposite the fact in dispute the nature of the dispute and describ[ing] the evidence that supports the position that the fact is controverted." Rule of Court 3.1350(f). It is not permitted to refer to another document for this information; the whole point of a reply separate statement is to put all of the evidence in one place for the Court. (See, e.g., Plaintiff's response and supporting evidence 1-13, 53, 56, 59, 61-63, 66-69, all referring to plaintiff's response to Defendant's Separate Statement of Undisputed Facts in Support of Motion for Summary Judgment, and to different paragraph numbers within that document.) Second, Plaintiff's 14 single spaced pages of objections to evidence do not follow the requirements of Rule of Court 3.1354. The objections do not directly "quote or set forth the objectionable statement or material." Rule of Court 3.1354. When a party objects to a statement in a declaration or deposition, the exact language should be quoted, not paraphrased, so the Court does not have to continually refer to other documents. Further, the objections should be succinct (see alternative formats permitted in Rule 3.1354(b)); if they are in a narrative form and as lengthy as these objections, they should be double spaced, not single spaced, so they can be read easily. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL BEAL et al VS. ROYAL OAK BAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 9, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 4, Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt And Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT ARES PAPAGEORGE'S APPLICATION FOR AN ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: CONTEMPT IS DENIED TO THE EXTENT THAT IT SEEKS RELIEF UNDER SECTION 1209 ET SQ. OF THE CALIFORNIA CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE CODE. AS NOTED IN THE COURT'S ORDER OF MARCH 12, 2013, THE ISSUES RAISED ARE DISCOVERY ISSUES AND AS SUCH COVERED BY OTHER SECTIONS OF THE CALIFORNIA CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, INCLUDING, SECTION 2023.030(e) WHICH PROVIDES FOR A CONTEMPT SANCTION IF APPROPRIATE. (SEE DISCUSSION BELOW 2). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. Counsel for Defendants and Cross-complainants shall prepare an appropriate order submit a copy to counsel for plaintiffs and cross-defendants for approval as to form and deliver a copy to the court at the hearing on April 9, 2013. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL BEAL et al VS. ROYAL OAK BAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Complainant Ares Papageorge'S Motion For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 9, 2013, Line 4, Cross Complainant Ares Papageorge'S Motion For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT ARES PAPAGEORGE'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS PURSUANT TO CCP §§ 2030.300(e), 2031.320(c), 2023.010(g) AND 2023.030 IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART WITH MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $12,000.00 IMPOSED AGAINST PLAINTIFFS AND CROSS-DEFENDANTS, MICHAEL BEAL AND ASHLEY JACKSON AND THEIR ATTORNEYS. TO THE EXTENT THAT THE MOTION SEEKS TERMINATING SANCTIONS, ISSUE SANCTIONS OR EVIDENCE SANCTIONS IT IS DENIED. TO THE EXTENT THAT IT SEEKS CONTEMPT SANCTIONS, INCLUDED WITHIN THE $12,000.00 IN MONETARY SANCTIONS IS $3,000.00 IN EXCESS OF REASONABLE ATTORNEYS' FEES AND COSTS. IN ADDITION, ALTHOUGH NOT SPECIFICALLY SOUGHT FOR IN THE RELIEF SET FORTH IN THE MOVING PAPERS THE COURT DEEMS THE MOTION TO ALSO SEEK COMPLIANCE WITH THE DISCOVERY REQUESTS PREVIOUSLY MADE TO PLAINTIFFS AND CROSS-DEFENDANTS. ACCORDINGLY, IT IS ORDERED AS FOLLOWS: (1) PLAINTIFFS AND CROSS-DEFENDANTS WILL FORTHWITH OBTAIN FROM ALL MEDICAL PROVIDERS IDENTIFIED IN THEIR INITIAL AND SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES COPIES OF THEIR MEDICAL RECORDS RELATING TO ANY TREATMENT THAT THEY ALLEGE WAS RELATED TO THE CLAIMS BEING ASSERTED AGAINST THE DEFENDANTS AND CROSS-DEFENDANTS AND DELIVER THOSE RECORDS TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS AND CROSS-DEFENDANTS WITHIN 10 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER; (2) WITHIN 10 FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER PLAINTIFFS AND CROSS-DEFENDANTS WILL PROVIDE TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS AND CROSS-COMPLAINANTS COPIES OF THEIR CREDIT CARD PURCHASES, IF ANY, MADE ON THE DAY OF THE INCIDENT AND COPIES OF THEIR CELL PHONE RECORDS REFLECTING ALL CALLS MADE FOR THE 24 HOUR PERIOD COMMENCING WITH 6:00 PM ON THE DAY OF THE INCIDENT AND, (3) FORTHWITH PROVIDE VERIFICATION OF THE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES DATED MARCH 7, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. Counsel for defendants and cross-complainants shall prepare an appropriate order submit a copy to counsel for plaintiffs and cross-defendants for approval as to form and deliver a copy to the court at the hearing on April 9, 2013.

rulings.Case Title: ELVIS BOKAN BY AND THROUGH HIS GUARDIAN AD LITEM, et al VS. THE BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA LIFE & HEALTH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion And Motion For Order Disqualifying Pltfs Designated Expert Witness And Pltfs Counsel For Conflict Of Interest
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 9, 2013, Line 5, DEFENDANT THE BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA LIFE & HEALTH'S Ntc Of Motion And Motion For Order Disqualifying Pltfs Designated Expert Witness And Pltfs Counsel For Conflict Of Interest Denied. "An attorney engaged in employment adverse to a former client is subject to disqualification where a 'substantial relationship' exists between the lawyer's current employment and the lawyer's representation of the former client." (Brand v. 20th Century Ins. Co./21st Century Ins. Co. (2004) 124 Cal.App.4th 594, 602.) The court finds that Mr. Prater was hired by Defendant as a mediator, expert witness, consultant, and perhaps as counsel for Defendant (although he never represented them directly in litigation). However, the court finds there is no substantial relationship between Mr. Prater's current employment and his representation of Defendant in any capacity. No evidence has been presented in the moving or opposing papers to show that Mr. Prater defended Defendant in any coverage or bad faith action or that his work involved the procedures and guidelines pertaining to the enrollment of newborn children and/or claims for newborn children, which is the subject of the instant action. Finally, the court finds that no confidential information pertaining to Defendant has been disclosed to Plaintiffs or their counsel. In his declaration, Brock D. Phillips, former general counsel for Defendant states that Defendant never provided any information to Mr. Prater which was intended to be confidential. There is no basis to disqualify counsel. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
